FT_IC1.evidence: Data from 9855 ABCD Study participants (ages 9-10 baseline, 21 U.S. sites, ABCD Release 3.0) explicitly stated throughout.

FT_IC5.evidence: Original empirical analysis using latent change score models on ABCD data to estimate effects of screen time types on intelligence at baseline and 2-year follow-up.

FT_IC6.evidence: Published peer-reviewed article in Scientific Reports (2022); no indication of duplicate.

FT_IC2.evidence: Step 1 hit: 'Socializing' (defined as texting + video chat + social networking sites — phone/SM-specific composite) has its own coefficient paired with intelligence (individual-trait). Baseline: r = -0.10, p < 0.001; longitudinal latent change model: β = -0.07, p = 0.220 (n.s.) but β = -0.10, p < 0.001 on baseline intelligence. Additionally, named platform items (texting on cell phone/tablet/computer; social networking e.g. Facebook, Twitter, Instagram) are explicitly listed as the Socializing items.

FT_IC3.evidence: Numerous quantitative estimates reported: Pearson r values at baseline (Socializing r = -0.10), standardized β coefficients from latent change score SEM models (e.g., Socializing β = -0.10 on baseline intelligence, p < 0.001; Gaming β = +0.17 on change in intelligence, p = 0.002).

FT_IC4.evidence: Inferential analyses performed: confirmatory factor analysis, latent change score SEM, multigroup gender analyses, mediation analyses — all with reported numeric results.

FT_EC7_reason: Screen time types (including Socializing = phone/SM variable) are used as IVs predicting intelligence (DV) in the latent change score models. SM/phone is IV, not DV-only. EC7 does not apply.
notes: Socializing = texting + video chat + visiting social networking sites; exclusively phone/SM items. Gaming is analyzed separately and independently. Paper also reports absolute gaming model (Fig. 2) with β = +0.21, p < 0.001 for gaming on change in intelligence.

FT_IC1.evidence: Participants from the ABCD cohort, 11,000+ children aged 9-11, recruited from 21 nationally distributed sites across the United States, ABCD Data Release 3.0 (https://nda.nih.gov/study.html?id=901).

FT_IC5.evidence: Original empirical analysis: cross-sectional linear regression, CLPM longitudinal analyses, and serial mediation analyses on ABCD genetic, neuroimaging, and behavioral data.

FT_IC6.evidence: Published peer-reviewed article in EBioMedicine (2022); no indication of duplicate.

FT_IC2.evidence: Step 1: No phone/SM-specific per-modality estimate found anywhere in the paper. STU (screen time utilization) is the sole screen variable and is defined in Methods as 'total amount of self-reported time using various electronic devices on both typical weekdays and weekend days (abcd_ssmty01), such as spending time on Facebook or watching movies' — a multi-device aggregate. No breakdown by cellphone, smartphone, or social media platform is reported in main results, supplementary tables, or sensitivity analyses. Step 2: The composite STU explicitly bundles smartphones, tablets, television, and computers without a per-device breakout — fails the Step 2 multi-device aggregation rule.

notes: STU is the ABCD variable abcd_ssmty01 which aggregates all electronic device time. Although Facebook is mentioned as an example activity, no Facebook-only or SM-only coefficient is reported. The paper's focus is on PRS-ADHD → white matter → STU pathway; STU is treated as DV in mediation models but the IC2 failure is the disqualifying issue.

FT_IC1.evidence: ABCD Study Release 3.0, N = 10,691 participants aged 9-10 years from 21 U.S. sites, explicitly stated.

FT_IC5.evidence: Original empirical analysis: JIVE dimension reduction plus generalized linear mixed models on ABCD baseline structural imaging and behavioral data.

FT_IC6.evidence: Published peer-reviewed article in Journal of Behavioral Addictions (2022); no indication of duplicate.

FT_IC2.evidence: Step 1: No phone/SM-specific per-modality estimate found anywhere. The paper reports only a single brain covariation component→total SMA score coefficient (β = 0.107, P = 0.002). SMA is never broken out by modality in any results table or supplement. Step 2: The SMA composite is defined in Methods as 'the total number of hours spent on all SMA activities' comprising: watching TV shows/movies, watching videos, playing video games, texting, visiting social networking sites, and video-chatting — this composite includes TV and video games alongside phone/SM items, without a separate per-modality breakout anywhere in the paper. Fails Step 2 multi-ingredient composite rule.

notes: SMA composite explicitly mixes TV, video games, texting, social networking, and video-chat into a single summed score. No sensitivity or supplementary table breaks out phone/SM-only items. The limitations section itself acknowledges 'the joint component here is associated with the total amount of SMA' rather than individual modalities.
